title: Expose the Face of Falsehood; Let Our New Government Blossom!
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the source's argument, political directness, rhetorical questions, repetition, irony, factual detail, and paragraph order. Names, dates, figures, quotations, and intentional English expressions are retained. Where Tamil idiom cannot be reproduced literally without sounding unnatural, the English follows its sense and rhetorical force without adding claims absent from the source. The original Tamil is reproduced in full below the translation and remains the authoritative text. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement. The source prints two deliberate repetitions: the questions on prohibition and MIDAS, and the concluding indictment of Jayalalithaa's politics of falsehood. Both are preserved in their printed positions. The Tamil reading copy below restores the source-visible salutation, list markers, the corrupted river-linking entry, broken words and figures, and other unambiguous readings confirmed against source pages 349-358. The canonical Tamil source remains unchanged.
paragraphs: No matter how clearly we answer Chief Minister Jayalalithaa's questions on prohibition, in terms anyone can understand, she does not stop speaking falsehoods about it. The Kazhagam's present election manifesto states:

> “A law will be enacted to implement prohibition in Tamil Nadu. Appropriate schemes will be implemented to compensate for the loss of government revenue resulting from the process of enforcing prohibition. The Tamil Nadu State Marketing Corporation (TASMAC) will be dissolved, and the sale of liquor now conducted through that corporation will be stopped completely.”

Yet on the issue of prohibition, the AIADMK minister responsible for the department declared categorically in the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly that prohibition could not be introduced even in stages. Every newspaper reported it. Later, during the present election campaign, Jayalalithaa contradicted her minister's Assembly statement and announced that prohibition would be introduced gradually in Tamil Nadu. Having made that half-hearted announcement, she now asks why the DMK manifesto says merely that a law will be enacted to implement prohibition in Tamil Nadu, why it does not explicitly say “total prohibition,” why it does not promise that this will be the first signature, and why the DMK has no intention of enforcing prohibition. She seems to have imagined all this and spoken simply for the sake of throwing out questions.

Her speech itself reveals how hard she works, again and again, to deceive and divert the people. Even after the DMK manifesto states clearly, “A law will be enacted to implement prohibition in Tamil Nadu. Appropriate schemes will be implemented to compensate for the loss of government revenue resulting from the process of enforcing prohibition,” Jayalalithaa, who has been driven by the general election to say that she will introduce prohibition gradually, fabricated a claim that I have no intention of implementing it and repeated that claim at an election meeting. Whatever else may be said, one thing is clear: Jayalalithaa herself has understood that we are going to win the election and take the seat of government!

If total prohibition is truly her policy, Jayalalithaa has been in office for the past five years. All authority has been in her hands! Did she lift even a blade of grass towards bringing in total prohibition, at least gradually? When the martyr Sasi Perumal died, and when women protested demanding the closure of TASMAC shops, did Jayalalithaa not merely stand by and watch? Who owns the MIDAS distillery? In whose name is it registered? Who were its directors, and who are they now? How much liquor was purchased from that company alone during the past five years? Who profited from it? Reports say that the company sells liquor directly to the bars! Is Chief Minister Jayalalithaa prepared to answer these questions at her next campaign meeting?

Jayalalithaa has also said: “As far as I am concerned, my policy is that total prohibition must be implemented. But it is not possible to bring in total prohibition with a single signature.” If total prohibition is truly her policy, Jayalalithaa has been in office for the past five years. All authority has been in her hands! Did she lift even a blade of grass towards bringing in total prohibition, at least gradually? When the martyr Sasi Perumal died, and when women protested demanding the closure of TASMAC shops, did Jayalalithaa not merely stand by and watch? Who owns the MIDAS distillery? In whose name is it registered? Who were its directors, and who are they now? How much liquor was purchased from that company alone during the past five years? Who profited from it? Reports say that the company sells liquor directly to the bars! Is Chief Minister Jayalalithaa prepared to answer these questions at her next campaign meeting?

Jayalalithaa has said, and her party newspaper has published, that “Karunanidhi and the DMK are lying when they claim to have fulfilled all the promises made in the DMK's 2006 election manifesto.” Why has the 2006 manifesto suddenly become necessary ten years later? Still, Jayalalithaa says that I made such a claim. Did the DMK promise in its 2006 manifesto to provide land to landless poor agricultural families, or did it not? Jayalalithaa asks why that promise was not fulfilled. Under the Kazhagam Government, 212,995 acres of free land were distributed to 179,002 families of landless poor agricultural labourers and farmers. Every farmer and agricultural labourer in Tamil Nadu knows this. Does Jayalalithaa not know that truth? Or does she think she can tell another lie about it? Is Jayalalithaa prepared to say how many farmers received how many acres of free land under the AIADMK Government during the five years after the Kazhagam administration?
Since Jayalalithaa has questioned the DMK manifesto, I wish to present the figures showing what the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam promised in its 2006 election manifesto and what it accomplished.
We promised twenty kilograms of rice every month at one rupee a kilogram. A total of 18.5 million families benefited from its implementation.
* Cooperative loans worth Rs 7,000 crore were waived for 2,240,739 farming families.
* After 2006, the Kazhagam Government provided crop loans totalling Rs 8,477 crore and 56 lakh to 3,554,721 farmers.
* A total of 117 Uzhavar Sandhais, farmers' markets, were revived with renewed vitality, and 45 new Uzhavar Sandhais were established.

* Under the crop insurance scheme, from 2006 the Government paid a 50 per cent subsidy on the insurance premium to encourage participation. As a result, compensation totalling Rs 974 crore was paid to 901,643 farmers.
* Loans obtained by Scheduled Caste farmers through the TAHDCO corporation up to 31 March 2006, totalling Rs 5 crore and 25 lakh including interest, were waived.
* Under the revolutionary scheme to link rivers flowing within the State, the Cauvery-Gundar river-linking project was undertaken at an estimated cost of Rs 189 crore.
* The Thamirabarani-Karumeniyar-Nambiyar river-linking project was undertaken at an estimated cost of Rs 369 crore.

* Under the scheme to organise farmers into self-help groups and provide revolving funds, 27,294 groups were formed and received Rs 27 crore, 29 lakh and 40 thousand as revolving funds. Crop loans totalling Rs 402 crore and 56 lakh were also given to 32,940 groups.
* A total of 21,155,525 members were enrolled in thirty-four unorganised workers' welfare boards, including the Agricultural Labourers Welfare Board.
* Assistance totalling Rs 995 crore, 78 lakh, 48 thousand and 815 was provided to 2,075,512 families of unorganised workers.
* Free colour television sets were distributed to 16,259,526 families at a cost of Rs 3,742 crore, 42 lakh and 59 thousand.
* Free gas stoves with gas connections were provided to 2.9 million families at a cost of Rs 661 crore.
* Free house-site pattas were given to 829,236 poor families.
* Kamarajar's birthday was observed in schools as “Education Development Day,” with educational celebrations.
* Eggs were served with nutritious meals five days a week to 7.3 million children and students; bananas were given to children who did not eat eggs.
* Government examination fees for Classes 10 and 12 were abolished for 1.1 million students.
* From 2010-2011, tuition fees were abolished for M.A. and M.Sc. courses as well.
* Free bus passes were issued annually to 2.482 million school students and 435,000 college students.
* Entrance examinations for professional courses were abolished.
* Under the policy of “one medical college for every district,” six new medical colleges were established at Villupuram, Tiruvarur, Dharmapuri, Sivaganga, Perambalur and Tiruvannamalai.
* A law was enacted making Tamil a compulsory subject in schools through Class 10.
* Fulfilling a century-old dream, the Central Institute of Classical Tamil was established in Chennai.
* Renovation work costing Rs 523 crore was carried out in 4,724 temples, followed by consecration ceremonies.
* Under the scheme providing Rs 6,000 each to poor pregnant women, a total of Rs 1,389 crore and 42 lakh was given to 2,576,612 women.
* Monthly assistance of Rs 500 was provided to 12,904 poor women over the age of fifty who remained unmarried and lived in poverty.
* The 108 emergency ambulance service was implemented throughout Tamil Nadu, benefiting 808,907 people.
* A medical insurance scheme was introduced for government employees.

* The Kalaignar Health Insurance Scheme for Life-Saving Treatments was implemented. A total of 13.4 million families were registered, and life-saving operations were performed for 255,744 poor people at a cost of Rs 667 crore.

* Twenty-seven memoranda of understanding involving investments of Rs 62,349 crore and expected to provide employment to approximately 235,464 people were signed. Action was taken through twenty-four government orders to establish fifty-one new factories; twelve of those factories had already opened.
* New government employment was provided to approximately 505,314 young people.
* The seawater desalination project was established at Minjur in North Chennai.
* After 2006, 175,493 new women's self-help groups, with 2.694 million members, were formed. Economic loans totalling Rs 1,068 crore were provided to them.
* Revolving funds totalling Rs 281 crore and 88 lakh were provided to 281,883 women's groups.
* The four municipalities of Erode, Tiruppur, Vellore and Thoothukudi were upgraded as municipal corporations.
* Ariyalur and Tiruppur emerged as new districts.
* A separate internal reservation of 3.5 per cent was provided for the advancement of the Muslim community.
* A separate internal reservation of 3 per cent was provided to relieve the deprivation of the Arunthathiyar community.
* In addition to the 145 Periyar Memorial Samathuvapurams already created so that people of every caste could live together in one place, another ninety-five Samathuvapurams were established.
* The world-class Anna Centenary Memorial Library, built at an estimated cost of Rs 181 crore in Kotturpuram, Chennai, was opened on 15 September 2010.
* Rs 910 crore was sanctioned for the new Legislative Assembly and Secretariat complex at the Omandurar Government Estate, and the complex was opened, marking an achievement.
* With financial assistance from the Japan Bank for International Cooperation, construction work on the Metro Rail Project was undertaken at an estimated cost of Rs 14,600 crore.
* The Hogenakkal Integrated Drinking Water Project was undertaken at an estimated cost of Rs 1,929 crore.
* The Ramanathapuram Integrated Drinking Water Project was completed at a cost of Rs 630 crore.
* Time-scale pay and pensions were provided for the benefit of 212,981 nutritious-meal workers.
* The recommendations of the Sixth Pay Commission were implemented for government employees and teachers, at an additional annual cost of Rs 5,155 crore and 79 lakh.
* The revolutionary Kalaignar Housing Scheme was implemented to convert 2.1 million huts into concrete houses within six years.
The DMK Government fulfilled innumerable promises, enough to keep listing one after another. We did what we said. Since Jayalalithaa asks, we are saying what we did!
But:
1. In its manifesto for the 2001 Assembly election, the AIADMK promised to fulfil forty-one pledges. Not one of them was fulfilled. What became of that election manifesto?
2. Of the promises Jayalalithaa made in her 2011 election manifesto, how many has she fulfilled to date? What are the details?
3. Under Rule 110 of the Legislative Assembly, Jayalalithaa read 187 statements up to 20 February 2016. How many of the announcements contained in them has she fulfilled so far? What are the details?

Jayalalithaa invests in lies, conducts a politics of lies, manufactures nothing but lies, distributes them, and keeps deceiving the innocent Tamil people. It would be far better for her to abandon the thought of setting a “Guinness” record for lying in politics; to avoid such “jokes” as “Spring is blowing across Tamil Nadu” and “I shall give you the government of your dreams”; to stop lying at least now; and to seek atonement for the lies she has told until today.

I have given detailed answers here to the questions Jayalalithaa asked about the election promises of 2006. She must likewise answer the three questions I have asked. Jayalalithaa invests in lies, conducts a politics of lies, manufactures nothing but lies, distributes them, and keeps deceiving the innocent Tamil people. It would be far better for her to abandon the thought of setting a “Guinness” record for lying in politics; to avoid such “jokes” as “Spring is blowing across Tamil Nadu” and “I shall give you the government of your dreams”; to stop lying at least now; and to seek atonement for the lies she has told until today.

Udanpirappē, I did not collect and set out all this information merely to answer Jayalalithaa's questions. I have presented it here because it will help you take these facts to our voters, explain them clearly, and strip away Jayalalithaa's lies. We have a duty to protect our people from the lies Jayalalithaa keeps flinging about and from their harmful consequences. I trust that you will discharge that duty with distinction. Expose the face of falsehood; our new government will blossom of its own accord! Victory is ours!
With affection,
M.K.
21 April 2016
